title: Unable to podman build inside docker
body:
/kind unknown

**Description**

On Ubuntu 20.04, I built podman image by Dockerfile: 

```
FROM ubuntu:groovy

RUN set -eux; \
    apt-get update -qq; \
    apt-get install podman runc ca-certificates < /dev/null > /dev/null; \
    ln -sf /usr/bin/podman /usr/bin/docker; \
    apt-get clean; \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists

COPY podman.policy.json /etc/containers/policy.json
```

and I used it to build another image:

```bash
docker run --privileged -v $PWD/repo:/repo podman bash
```
Inside container, it failed to build image:

```
cd /repo
podman build -t test .

STEP 1: FROM ubuntu:bionic
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob b1e4d03d773f done  
Copying config 841b4771a7 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Error: error mounting new container: error mounting build container "b9cfb96e1542cd957f8491fa2415e370b1b052e7054fa46d8bb389613e7c8394": error creating aufs mount to /var/lib/containers/storage/aufs/mnt/5463d63c5bbb090dc93c7290654d2dd6c5bee4f86813276b5d0228b65b18fb7f: invalid argument
```

In additional, `docker info`:

```
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.15.2
  cgroupVersion: v1
  conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon'
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.20, commit: unknown'
  cpus: 8
  distribution:
    distribution: ubuntu
    version: "20.10"
  eventLogger: file
  hostname: ba3375fca3ad
  idMappings:
    gidmap: null
    uidmap: null
  kernel: 5.4.0-54-generic
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 5340209152
  memTotal: 16692097024
  ociRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: 'runc: /usr/sbin/runc'
    path: /usr/sbin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/podman/podman.sock
  rootless: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: ""
    package: ""
    version: ""
  swapFree: 792051712
  swapTotal: 792051712
  uptime: 10h 0m 28.08s (Approximately 0.42 days)
registries: {}
store:
  configFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 0
    paused: 0
    running: 0
    stopped: 0
  graphDriverName: aufs
  graphOptions: {}
  graphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: overlayfs
    Dirperm1 Supported: "false"
    Dirs: "0"
    Root Dir: /var/lib/containers/storage/aufs
  imageStore:
    number: 0
  runRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  volumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
version:
  APIVersion: 1
  Built: 0
  BuiltTime: Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
  GitCommit: ""
  GoVersion: go1.14.7
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 2.0.6
```

@likema: The label(s) `kind/unknown` cannot be applied, because the repository doesn't have them

If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

Could you try mounting a volume on /var/lib/containers
And see if this fixes your issue.

BTW:
Should
cd /repo
docker build -t test .
Be
cd /repo
podman build -t test .

> Could you try mounting a volume on /var/lib/containers
> And see if this fixes your issue.

How to mount it manually?

> BTW:
> Should
> cd /repo
> docker build -t test .
> Be
> cd /repo
> podman build -t test 

I forgot to describe that  

```
ln -s /usr/bin/podman /usr/bin/docker
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since I gave an example on how to do this, and never heard any more, I am going to assume this issue is solved.

> mkdir containers
> docker run -v $PWD/containers:/var/lib/containers ...

I can not mount `$PWD/containers` to `/var/lib/containers` manually, because podman image is running inside docker by gitlab runner.

Same issue:
```
Error: error mounting new container: error mounting build container "87d13c44b51692513aeee1d955d974a949ce8bdb0eafdbbdac65bd39badb0e4d": error creating aufs mount to /var/lib/containers/storage/aufs/mnt/6331b6fe346071788da2bdd391dce34d034caec557853babdb96bb66723c23a4: invalid argument
```
This looks like an issue with the aufs storage backend?
Same problems here. I would like to run podman inside gitlab ci pipeline and get the same error.

I solved this by running Podman on the agent itself. I was using [JFrog-Pipelines](https://jfrog.com/pipelines/) with a custom docker image on the agent which caused this problem.
Try to install Podman directly on the agent and then build the image.

> Could you try mounting a volume on /var/lib/containers

Faced the same issue and your advice helped me
